elements.sent-2411-001: The perturbative contribution to the correlator of two HQET heavy-light currents expanded in light-quark masses up to quadratic terms is calculated up to 4 loops.
elements.sent-2411-002: It has been calculated at 2~\cite{Broadhurst:1991fc,Bagan:1991sg,Neubert:1991sp and 3~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd loops.}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Here we consider the perturbative contribution at four loops.
elements.sent-2411-004: We closely follow notations of~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd ; properties of this correlator are discussed there in detail.}
elements.sent-2411-005: We consider the correlator $\Pi_P(\tau)$, where $P=\pm1$ is the parity of the current $j_P$ (see~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd ), and $\tau$ is the Euclidean time.}
elements.sent-2411-006: The operator product expansion (OPE) is \begin{equation}\Pi_P(\tau) = \sum_{\mathcal{O}} C_{\mathcal{O}}(\tau) \langle\mathcal{O}\rangle\,. \label{OPE}\end{equation} We expand in light-quark masses, so that these masses are included in the operators.
elements.sent-2411-007: Wilson coefficients of even-dimensional operators $\mathcal{O}$ don't depend on $P$; those of odd-dimensional operators are proportional to $P$.
elements.sent-2411-008: Up to dimension 2, there are 4 operators: $\mathcal{O = 1$, $m$, $m^2$, $\sum m_i^2$, where $m$ is the mass of the quark field in our current, and $m_i$ are the masses of all light flavors.}
elements.sent-2411-009: At dimension 3, there is the light-quark condensate and cubic mass combinations; they mix under renormalization, and cannot be considered separately.
elements.sent-2411-010: In the present note we consider only dimensions up to 2.
elements.sent-2411-011: We use the same calculation framework as in~\cite{Grozin:2023dlk .}
elements.sent-2411-012: Diagrams are generated by qgraf~\cite{Nogueira:1991ex ; Dirac traces and contractions are calculated by form~\cite{Kuipers:2012rf,Ruijl:2017dtg ; color factors are calculated by the form package color~\cite{vanRitbergen:1998pn .}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: IBP reduction is done by LiteRed~\cite{Lee:2012cn,Lee:2013mka version 2, the master integrals expanded in $\varepsilon$ have been obtained in~\cite{Lee:2022art .}}
elements.sent-2411-015: We did the calculation exactly in the covariant-gauge parameter $\xi$ up to 3 loops, and up to $\xi^1$ at 4 loops.
elements.sent-2411-016: Cancelation of gauge-dependent terms is a strong check of our calculation.
elements.sent-2411-017: The correlator and operators $\mathcal{O}$ (including $m$, $m_i$) are renormalized in the $\overline{\text{MS }$ scheme at some $\mu$.}
elements.sent-2411-018:
Evolution of the coefficient functions $C_{m^n (	au)$ for $0 \le n \le 2$ is governed by the anomalous dimension  egin{equation}\gamma_n = 2 \gamma_j - n \gamma_m\,. \label{gamman}\end{equation} The renormalization-group structure of these coefficients is  ootnote{Note a typo in~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd , formula~(34): the formula is correct if $\gamma_n$ is defined as $\gamma_j- rac{n {2}\gamma_m$, not as~(ef{gamman ).  egin{eqnarray} C_{m^n}(	au) =  rac{N_c C_0^{(m^n)}}{\pi^2 	au^{3-n}} \exp iggl\{  rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} (- \gamma_{n,0} L_	au + c_1^{(m^n)}) 
onumber {} +  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!2}  igl[-  eta_0 \gamma_{n,0} L_	au^2 + (- \gamma_{n,1} + 2  eta_0 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au + c_2^{(m^n)} igr] 
onumber {} +  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!3}  iggl[-  rac{4}{3}  eta_0^2 \gamma_{n,0} L_	au^3 + (- 2  eta_0 \gamma_{n,1} -  eta_1 \gamma_{n,0} + 4  eta_0^2 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au^2 
onumber \quad{} + (- \gamma_{n,2} + 4  eta_0 c_2^{(m^n)} + 2  eta_1 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au + c_3^{(m^n)} iggr] + \cdots  iggr\}\,, \label{RG}\end{eqnarray} where  egin{eqnarray} L_	au = \log rac{\mu 	au e^{\gamma_E}}{2}\,,\quad j_0 = Z_j j\,,\quad m_0 = Z_m m\,, 
onumber \gamma_a =  rac{d\log Z_a}{d\log\mu} = \sum_{l=1}^\infty \gamma_{a,l-1}  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!l}\quad (a = j, m)\,, 
onumber  eta = -  rac{1}{2}  rac{d\log\alpha_s}{d\log\mu} = \sum_{l=1}^\infty  eta_{l-1}  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!l}\,. \label{Lt}\end{eqnarray} We need $\gamma_j$ up to 3 loops~\cite{Chetyrkin:2003vi (recently is has been calculated at 4 loops~\cite{Grozin:2023dlk ); $\gamma_m$ up to 3 loops and $ eta$ up to 2 loops are well known.}}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-003:
egin{equation}C_{m^n}(	au) =  rac{N_c C_0^{(m^n)}}{\pi^2 	au^{3-n}} \exp iggl\{  rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} (- \gamma_{n,0} L_	au + c_1^{(m^n)}) 
onumber {} +  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!2}  igl[-  eta_0 \gamma_{n,0} L_	au^2 + (- \gamma_{n,1} + 2  eta_0 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au + c_2^{(m^n)} igr] 
onumber {} +  iggl( rac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} iggr)^{\!\!3}  iggl[-  rac{4}{3}  eta_0^2 \gamma_{n,0} L_	au^3 + (- 2  eta_0 \gamma_{n,1} -  eta_1 \gamma_{n,0} + 4  eta_0^2 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au^2 
onumber \quad{} + (- \gamma_{n,2} + 4  eta_0 c_2^{(m^n)} + 2  eta_1 c_1^{(m^n)}) L_	au + c_3^{(m^n)} iggr] + \cdots  iggr\}\,, \label{RG}\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-004: \begin{equation}L_\tau = \log\frac{\mu \tau e^{\gamma_E}}{2}\,,\quad j_0 = Z_j j\,,\quad m_0 = Z_m m\,, \nonumber \gamma_a = \frac{d\log Z_a}{d\log\mu} = \sum_{l=1}^\infty \gamma_{a,l-1} \biggl(\frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi}\biggr)^{\!\!l}\quad (a = j, m)\,, \nonumber \beta = - \frac{1}{2} \frac{d\log\alpha_s}{d\log\mu} = \sum_{l=1}^\infty \beta_{l-1} \biggl(\frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi}\biggr)^{\!\!l}\,. \label{Lt}\end{equation}

elements.formula-2411-006: \begin{equation}C_{\sum m_i^2}(\tau) = \frac{N_c}{\pi^2} \frac{\sum m_i^2}{\tau} C_F T_F \biggl(\frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi}\biggr)^{\!\!2} \Bigl\{c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} \nonumber {} + \frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} \Bigl[ (-\gamma_{2,0} + 4 \beta_0) c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} L_\tau + c_1^{(\sum m_i^2)} \Bigr] + \cdots\Bigr\}\,. \label{RG2}\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-007: \begin{equation}c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} = - \frac{2}{3} (\pi^2 - 6)\,,\quad c_1^{(\sum m_i^2)} = 4 C_F \biggl(18 \zeta_3 + \frac{\pi^4}{9} - \frac{7}{3} \pi^2 - 5\biggr) \nonumber {} - C_A \biggl(30 \zeta_5 - 2 \pi^2 \zeta_3 - 110 \zeta_3 + \frac{197}{5670} \pi^6 + \frac{4}{45} \pi^4 + \frac{226}{27} \pi^2 \nonumber {} - \frac{350}{9}\biggr) - 32 T_F n_f \biggl(\zeta_3 - \frac{\pi^2}{27} - \frac{1}{9}\biggr)\,. \label{sm2}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-021: The $C_A$ term in $c_1^{(\sum m_i^2)}$ contains diagrams with a light-quark triangle; the $C_F$ and $T_F n_f$ terms contain only simler diaghrams with 2-point light-quark insertions to gluon propagators.
elements.sent-2411-022: In the momentum-space correlator of the renormalized current $j$ \begin{equation}\Pi_P(\omega) = \sum_{\mathcal{O}} C_{\mathcal{O}}(\omega) \langle\mathcal{O}\rangle \label{OPE2}\end{equation} the coefficient functions $C_{\mathcal{O (\omega)$ contain $1/\varepsilon^k$ poles, see~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd .}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: These poles contain only $\omega^{2-n}$ (where $n \le 2$ is the dimensionality of $\mathcal{O}$), no logarithms of $\omega$.
elements.sent-2411-024: Hence the corresponding $1/\varepsilon^k$ contributions to the coordinate-space correlator $\Pi_P(t)$ are proportional to $\delta^{(2-n) (t)$.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The Euclidean correlator $\Pi_P(\tau)$~(\ref{OPE ) is obtained by analytical continuation from $t>0$ to $t = i\tau$, $\tau>0$, and such $\delta$ terms are dropped.}
elements.sent-2411-026: When we calculate the spectral density, polynomials in $\omega$ have no discontinuity, and the result is also finite.
elements.sent-2411-027: Cancelation of $1/\varepsilon^k$ poles in the renormalized $\Pi_P(\tau)$ and $\rho_P(\omega)$ is a strong check of our calculation.
elements.sent-2411-028: The Wilson coefficients $C_{\mathcal{O (\omega)$ are in the attached file; notations are explained in the comment at the top of this file.}}
elements.sent-2411-029: The spectral density is \begin{equation}\rho_P(\omega) = \sum_{\mathcal{O}} R_{\mathcal{O}}(\omega) \langle\mathcal{O}\rangle\,. \label{OPE3}\end{equation} The renormalization-group structure of the coefficient functions $R_{m^n (\omega)$ for $0 \le n \le 2$ is given by~(\ref{RG ) with the substitutions \begin{eqnarray} C_0^{(m^n)} \to R_0^{(m^n)}\,,\quad c_i^{(m^n)} \to r_i^{(m^n)}\,, \frac{1}{\tau^{3-n}} \to \omega^{2-n}\,,\quad L_\tau \to L_\omega = \log\frac{\mu}{2\omega}\end{eqnarray} (note that $L_\omega$ in~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd is defined with the opposite sign).}}}

onumber r^{(m)}_3 = c^{(m)}_3 - 2 C_F  iggl[ C_F^2  iggl(252 \zeta_3 +  rac{40}{9} \pi^4 -  rac{70}{3} \pi^2 - 359 iggr) + C_F C_A  iggl(722 \zeta_3 +  rac{452}{27} \pi^4 +  rac{2753}{27} \pi^2 -  rac{15718}{9} iggr) 
onumber \quad{} +  rac{C_A^2}{9}  iggl(9526 \zeta_3 +  rac{502}{9} \pi^4 +  rac{5507}{9} \pi^2 - 27238 iggr) - 2 C_F T_F n_f  iggl(176 \zeta_3 +  rac{448}{135} \pi^4 +  rac{380}{27} \pi^2 -  rac{3637}{9} iggr) 
onumber \quad{} -  rac{4}{9} C_A T_F n_f  iggl(1354 \zeta_3 +  rac{458}{45} \pi^4 +  rac{1007}{9} \pi^2 - 4553 iggr) +  rac{16}{3} (T_F n_f)^2  iggl( rac{64}{3} \zeta_3 +  rac{4}{27} \pi^4 +  rac{47}{27} \pi^2 - 59 iggr)  iggr]\,, \label{r1} R^{(m^2)}_0 = -  rac{1}{8}\,,\quad r^{(m^2)}_1 = c^{(m^2)}_1\,,\quad r^{(m^2)}_2 = c^{(m^2)}_2 - \pi^2 C_F (27 C_F + 11 C_A - 4 T_F n_f)\,, 
onumber r^{(m^2)}_3 = c^{(m^2)}_3 - C_F  iggl[ C_F^2 (1944 \zeta_3 + 16 \pi^4 + 3 \pi^2) + 2 C_F C_A  iggl(1188 \zeta_3 +  rac{34}{27} \pi^4 +  rac{1693}{9} \pi^2 iggr) +  rac{C_A^2}{3}  iggl(1936 \zeta_3 -  rac{44}{9} \pi^4 +  rac{1681}{3} \pi^2 iggr) 
onumber \quad{} - 4 C_F T_F n_f  iggl(216 \zeta_3 +  rac{16}{27} \pi^4 +  rac{271}{9} \pi^2 iggr) -  rac{4}{3} C_A T_F n_f  iggl(352 \zeta_3 -  rac{4}{9} \pi^4 +  rac{269}{3} \pi^2 iggr) + 16 (T_F n_f)^2  iggl( rac{16}{3} \zeta_3 + \pi^2 iggr)  iggr]\,. \label{r2}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-031: For $\sum m_i^2$ we have \begin{eqnarray} R_{\sum m_i^2}(\omega) = \frac{N_c}{\pi^2} \Bigl(\sum m_i^2\Bigr) C_F T_F \biggl(\frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi}\biggr)^{\!\!2} \Bigl\{c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} \nonumber {} + \frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} \Bigl[ (-\gamma_{2,0} + 4 \beta_0) c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} L_\omega + c_1^{(\sum m_i^2)} \Bigr] + \cdots\Bigr\}\,, \label{RG3}\end{eqnarray} where $c_i^{(\sum m_i^2)}$ are given by~(\ref{sm2 ).}
elements.formula-2411-012: \begin{equation}R_{\sum m_i^2}(\omega) = \frac{N_c}{\pi^2} \Bigl(\sum m_i^2\Bigr) C_F T_F \biggl(\frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi}\biggr)^{\!\!2} \Bigl\{c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} \nonumber {} + \frac{\alpha_s}{4\pi} \Bigl[ (-\gamma_{2,0} + 4 \beta_0) c_0^{(\sum m_i^2)} L_\omega + c_1^{(\sum m_i^2)} \Bigr] + \cdots\Bigr\}\,, \label{RG3}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-032: The first term agrees with~\cite{Chetyrkin:2021qvd , the second one is new.}
elements.sent-2411-033: To conclude: we have calculated the perturbative contribution to the correlator of HQET heavy-light quark currents (expanded in light-quark masses up to quadratic terms) with the 4-loop accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-034: They can be used in QCD sum rules for heavy-light mesons; in particular, the terms with light-quark masses are useful for $B_s^{(*)}$ and its differences from $B^{(*)}$: they describe the breaking of the $SU(3)$ flavor symmetry.
elements.sent-2411-035: These correlators can also be used for comparisons with results of lattice simulations.
elements.sent-2411-036: The work was supported by the Russian Science Foundation, grant number 20-12-00205.. \end{acknowledgments.}
